So this video is going to show you how to optimize your ad set budget campaigns. So before we get started, I want to show you how to tell if you're using an ad set budget campaign or a CBO campaign and kind of cover the differences between the two. An ad set budget campaign allows you to set a budget for each of your ads individually. You could have some ads at $5, $10 and they can all be separate. While a CBO budget campaign, you set a overall budget like $40 on this one, for example. And Facebook will automatically spread that money out through your ads, how they see fit and how they determine whether which ad will perform well or not, and try to find the best performing ads. As you can see here on the budget side of underneath this column, you can tell that it says using ad set budget or it has a number showing the full campaign budget. That's probably the easiest way to tell if you're on the campaign section of Facebook Ads Manager. For this example, we're going to use the free 42-day detox campaign. This is an older campaign, so you probably won't be running any free ads, but this is just for example. And all of these ads are turned off as they are, you know, quite old. So don't let that kind of fool you. Your ads will most likely be turned on while you're making these changes. So the first couple of things we want to look at, and your kind of KPIs or key performance indicators, are really your cost per result is your main one. So if we look at this, we can see that we have $10, $20, $7 and $6 cost per result. And this is how much you're paying for each lead that comes through. So this is determined by the amount spent divided by the number of results. An average cost per result is anywhere between $8 and $13. If you're in this range, that's good, that's fine, that's pretty much an average ad. If you're below this, you're doing really well. Those are ads you want to focus on and that you definitely want to keep live. I would say around the $15 mark is where I start to look to turn those ads off and replace them with new ads. Looking through this, we can see that we have two down here that are $6 and $7 respectively. These are two ads that you would want to focus on when optimizing your ads. So if you came in here, they're all set to a $5 budget and you want to take the $6 and $7 ads. You can just go to edit on either one of them. If you scroll down, budget, here's where you'll find the daily budget. Because these ads are doing so well and they're decently below the average, I would recommend just raising it up to $10. Honestly, I try to stick around the $5 increment whenever I raise my ads. I don't like making too big of jumps, but I'll check this every other day or every day to see if they're still doing well. If they continue to do well, you can keep raising the daily budget. Once you've increased the budget to where you see fit, you want to go down and hit publish, and this will publish that ad. You would then want to repeat this process for any of the other ads that are below the average cost per result or are bringing in a high number of leads. There are other statistics that can really help you with this that aren't quite as important as cost per result. If we look at reach and impressions, this will show us the reach is the exact number of people that have been shown the ad, while the impressions are the amount of times it has been interacted with, viewed for over 3 seconds, or clicked, commented, shared, etc. Just to cover a couple other things to show you what to do here in situations when optimizing your ads. You can also view charts underneath your advertisements. If you click this button, it will open up and show you performance, and this will allow you to see a better breakdown, or a more detailed breakdown at least, of what is going on with your ad, including when they opted in, and the frequency, the amount spent, and the number of people reached. But the ones I really like under here is looking at the demographics. Here you can see the age ranges of the people that's opting in to your ad. This lets you know what age ranges you want to focus on for your next ads and moving forward. You can also see the placements to see where the leads are coming from and where they're being shown the ad that is converting the best. So in this case, it was only shown on Facebook. But if you had other placements such as Instagram, Audience Network, etc., you would be able to see which one is doing the best. This will help you in the future when creating new ads.
